role: LLM abstraction and orchestration layer for the ArchSpine mirror system, providing a unified interface to interact with various language model providers.
responsibility: Defines contracts (LLMClient, LLMTransport, etc.) for interacting with external LLMs, implements provider-specific transports (OpenAI, Gemini, mock), manages global and project-level configuration and secrets, resolves runtime LLM settings, generates and parses prompts for different file kinds (config, document, folder, project, source), and includes retry logic with exponential backoff for transient errors — collectively enabling AI-driven architectural evaluation and documentation generation.

children.role: Interface definitions for the LLM abstraction layer, establishing the contract between ArchSpine and external language model providers.

Infrastructure factory implementing the Factory Method pattern to instantiate provider-specific LLM transport clients. Acts as the central point for provider selection and initialization in the ArchSpine LLM subsystem.

Global LLM configuration and secrets manager for the ArchSpine CLI tool. Provides platform-aware directory detection, persistent config loading/saving, and credential management via a CredentialStore.

LLM orchestration service implementing the LLMClient interface. Coordinates prompt generation, LLM transport calls, and structured response parsing for multiple file kinds (config, document, folder, project, source).
Provides concrete LLM transport implementations and testing utilities for the ArchSpine mirror system.
Retry utility with exponential backoff for transient LLM provider errors.

LLM runtime configuration resolution layer. It bridges project-level and global-level configuration with secrets to produce a validated, ready-to-use LLM client instance. Encapsulates the resolution logic and validation.
